title: Share of local governments that implemented local disaster risk reduction strategies in line with national strategies
caption:
Share of local governments that implemented local disaster risk reduction strategies in line with national strategies

This choropleth world map displays the share of local governments that implemented local disaster risk reduction strategies in line with national strategies, as of 2024. The legend ranges from 0% to 80%, with color bands marking 20%, 40%, 60%, and 80% thresholds, alongside a hatched pattern indicating no data. The darkest blue shade, representing values above 80%, covers large portions of North America, including the United States and Canada, as well as Russia, China, and several countries in South America such as Argentina and Brazil. In Europe, nations like Spain, France, and Germany also appear in this highest category. Conversely, a significant portion of the African continent, along with Greenland and parts of the Middle East, features the hatched no-data pattern. Among the countries with reported data in Africa, South Africa and Botswana sit in the teal band between 40% and 60%, while Egypt and Morocco appear in the darkest blue band. Australia is colored in teal, placing it in the 40% to 60% range. India and Indonesia appear in lighter green shades, falling within the 20% to 40% band. Several countries in West Africa and the Middle East, such as Saudi Arabia and Niger, are colored in pale yellow, indicating shares between 0% and 20%. The visual distribution shows high implementation shares across the Americas, Europe, and parts of Asia, while data is missing for many nations in Africa and the Middle East.
